# H036 Global Public-World Solver HS-JEPA

## Question

Can the full set of known public LB observations be explained by a latent public subset plus a row/target binary label world, and can that posterior justify a nonlocal post-H012 move?

## Sensor facts

- known public sensors used: 21

- anchor: `submission_h012_public_equation_top_all_k1200_a0.7_uploadsafe.csv` = 0.5681234831

- observed files explained relative to H012: 20

## World fit

- best particle MAE=0.000202825, RMSE=0.000249943, Spearman=0.970

- permutation-null p(best_null_mae <= real_best_mae)=0.000

## Interpretation
- If the permutation-null p-value is high, many fake public worlds fit the observations just as well; the world posterior is not a reliable new target.
- If H024/H025 reject the best world move, the public-equation posterior is still not translated into an action-health-safe submission.
- Promotion requires all gates because H012 already sits in a narrow basin and local stress failures have been common after H012.
